jokes.text: Don't eat too many, okay, Daddy?
jokes.setup: Phil says he's going to check that Geoffrey put out cookies for Santa
jokes.punchline: Young Nicky immediately assumes his dad will eat them himself
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment that establishes Phil's eating habits through child's innocent perspective

jokes.text: Say, Uncle Phil, can I borrow 50 bucks, man? Of course, some things never change.
jokes.setup: Phil narrates that 'a lot of things were different back then'
jokes.punchline: Will immediately asks for money, proving some things are eternal
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that immediately establishes continuity and character consistency

jokes.text: You know, these popcorn strings are looking a little skimpy. But you wouldn't know nothing about that would you, Uncle Phil?
jokes.setup: Will notices the popcorn decorations look sparse
jokes.punchline: Implies Phil has been eating the decorations
jokes.explanation: Standard fat joke elevated by Will's delivery style and character dynamic

jokes.text: I don't know what you're talking about. And I suppose this is the one-armed man The Fugitive was looking for, huh?
jokes.setup: Phil denies eating popcorn
jokes.punchline: References The Fugitive while presumably holding evidence of his snacking
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture reference that works on multiple levels - denial plus visual evidence

jokes.text: Hey, little fellow, Santa let you out of the workshop early?
jokes.setup: Someone enters (presumably Carlton in Christmas attire)
jokes.punchline: Will mistakes him for an elf due to his height
jokes.explanation: Standard height joke but fits characters and situation well

jokes.text: Oh, you like my outfit? I had jingle bells sewn into my cuffs.
jokes.setup: Carlton responds to the elf comment
jokes.punchline: He's genuinely proud of his overly festive outfit details
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - his earnest enthusiasm makes the joke

jokes.text: Oh, but it's all right for him to think Santa's only three feet tall.
jokes.setup: Phil wants his Santa suit pressed so Nicky thinks Santa looks sharp
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey points out the height inconsistency with dry wit
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery with perfect logic pointing out the absurdity

jokes.text: I was mauled in the Saks shoe department. You need a license to drive, but they'll let anyone shop.
jokes.setup: Someone asks what happened to Hilary's coat
jokes.punchline: She dramatically describes shopping as warfare with social commentary
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary - privileged perspective with genuinely clever observation about licensing

jokes.text: Children, there is no point in competing over who got Nicky the best present. That's right, because you'll all lose to me.
jokes.setup: Viv starts to give mature advice about not competing
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals she's the most competitive of all
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection that subverts expectation of parental wisdom

jokes.text: Hey there, G I don't mean to be rude But take your jelly-roll butt And go get me some food
jokes.setup: Will presents his christening gift
jokes.punchline: It's a crude rap puppet that insults Geoffrey
jokes.explanation: Typical Will inappropriate humor - fits character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: So that's where the cereal box tops went.
jokes.setup: Will shows off his handmade puppet
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey dryly identifies the cheap materials used
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's perfect deadpan response that deflates Will's pride

jokes.text: That is a hand puppet.
jokes.setup: Will defends his gift as 'great'
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey states the obvious with devastating understatement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey deadpan - simple but devastating in its understatement

jokes.text: He's so cheap, he'd probably make Nicky a vest out of the lint from his pocket or something.
jokes.setup: Will says he can count on Carlton to make him look good
jokes.punchline: Predicts Carlton will give an even cheaper gift made of pocket lint
jokes.explanation: Setup for Carlton's entrance - absurd image but not particularly original

jokes.text: Hey, is Nicky gonna love this or what?
jokes.setup: Will just predicted Carlton would give a cheap gift
jokes.punchline: Carlton enters with obvious pride in what's presumably an elaborate/expensive gift
jokes.explanation: Good setup payoff that subverts Will's prediction about Carlton being cheap

jokes.text: Oh, man. Yo, me and this kid are definitely blood.
jokes.setup: Nicky responds positively to music/dancing
jokes.punchline: Will claims genetic connection based on shared musical taste
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment that establishes Will's bond with baby Nicky

jokes.text: Santa left it in the men's room.
jokes.setup: Jazz shows up with an expensive Rolex for the baby
jokes.punchline: Claims Santa left it in a bathroom (obvious theft implication)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jazz moment - obvious lie delivered with complete confidence

jokes.text: I got him Boyz II Men, man. I used to hang with them back in Philly.
jokes.setup: Will is embarrassed by his cheap gift compared to Jazz's Rolex
jokes.punchline: Makes impossible claim about getting the famous group to perform
jokes.explanation: Classic Will overcompensation that sets up the episode's main conflict

jokes.text: Come back here! Well, this is a christening your family won't forget. Come back here!
jokes.setup: Vivian runs off excited to tell everyone about Boyz II Men
jokes.punchline: Will's panicked chasing and ominous prediction about unforgettable christening
jokes.explanation: Good escalation moment that builds tension while being funny

jokes.text: Hey, you know, cheese steaks, Liberty Bell, man, West Philly!
jokes.setup: Will is trying to convince Boyz II Men he knows them from Philly
jokes.punchline: Desperately lists obvious Philadelphia references
jokes.explanation: Good desperation comedy showing Will grasping for connection

jokes.text: Listen, I stole her from this old four-eyed, big-lipped dude, right? Used to be singing these stupid songs on the corner.
jokes.setup: Will is trying to impress by talking about stealing someone's girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Unknowingly insults the person he's talking to about their early career
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - dramatic irony where Will unknowingly destroys himself

jokes.text: I cried myself to sleep every night about that. Did I say I was from Philly? I'm from Pittsburgh, man. Go, Steelers.
jokes.setup: Nate reveals Will broke his heart over the girl
jokes.punchline: Will desperately tries to deny his entire identity
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of the cringe - Will's complete identity denial is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Where's your Christmas spirit? You're a nun for God's sake.
jokes.setup: Will is begging Nicie (now apparently a nun) to help him
jokes.punchline: Appeals to Christmas spirit while literally invoking God to a nun
jokes.explanation: Decent desperation comedy with mild religious humor twist

jokes.text: What am I gonna get him, a mop?
jokes.setup: Ashley suggests they get Geoffrey a present
jokes.punchline: Hilary can only think of cleaning supplies as appropriate for the help
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment showing her oblivious class insensitivity

jokes.text: Oh, yes, I did. And I guess you found it.
jokes.setup: Vivian compliments Vy on losing weight
jokes.punchline: Vy implies Vivian found the lost weight
jokes.explanation: Classic comeback structure with perfect timing and economy - predictable but well-executed

jokes.text: And I guess you found it.
jokes.setup: Vivian asks if Vy lost weight and compliments her
jokes.punchline: Vy immediately implies Vivian gained the weight Vy lost
jokes.explanation: Classic fat joke but delivered with perfect timing and sisterly snark

jokes.text: Okay, now I remember where Will gets it from, right?
jokes.setup: Vy delivers a cutting remark to Vivian
jokes.punchline: Phil connects Will's sarcastic nature to his mother's influence
jokes.explanation: Nice character connection that explains Will's behavior through genetics

jokes.text: How come ain't nobody tell me Halle Berry was going to be up in here?
jokes.setup: Will enters and sees Vy for the first time
jokes.punchline: Compares his mother to a famous actress (inappropriate but flattering)
jokes.explanation: Typical Will inappropriate but sweet compliment to his mother

jokes.text: Your generosity knows no bounds, sir.
jokes.setup: Phil grudgingly says he'll answer the door
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey sarcastically comments on Phil's 'generosity'
jokes.explanation: Classic Geoffrey sarcasm delivered with perfect British understatement

jokes.text: Oh, damn.
jokes.setup: Vy says she'll brag about Will getting Boyz II Men for the christening
jokes.punchline: Will realizes how much deeper his lie has gotten
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction showing Will's growing panic

jokes.text: You know what? You're right, Carlton. You're a genius, man.
jokes.setup: Carlton gives generic cheerful advice about Santa
jokes.punchline: Will sarcastically calls Carlton a genius (but actually gets an idea)
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's sarcasm plus actual inspiration

jokes.text: Honey, I think your wig is on too tight.
jokes.setup: Helen makes a cutting remark about not wanting to look like twins
jokes.punchline: Vy suggests the wig is affecting Helen's brain function
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that's both personal and cleverly constructed

jokes.text: When you hear this story, you are gonna laugh.
jokes.setup: Vivian approaching two angry women
jokes.punchline: Promising they'll find her mistake hilarious when they clearly won't
jokes.explanation: Good ironic setup - audience knows this won't go well

jokes.text: It's stolen.
jokes.setup: Phil discovers his Santa suit is missing
jokes.punchline: His dramatic declaration in the middle of family chaos
jokes.explanation: Adds to the chaos but relatively simple comedy beat

jokes.text: Mind the booth. Gotta tinkle.
jokes.setup: Will approaches mall Santa booth
jokes.punchline: Mall Santa leaves Will in charge while going to bathroom
jokes.explanation: Setup for Will's Santa impersonation - crude but functional

jokes.text: So much food, you'll think a supermarket blew up.
jokes.setup: Boyz II Men ask about reception food
jokes.punchline: Will promises excessive amounts with colorful metaphor
jokes.explanation: Decent Will-style exaggeration that convinces the group

jokes.text: Yo, that's that fool that stole Nicie.
jokes.setup: Will successfully convinces Boyz II Men as Santa
jokes.punchline: Nate recognizes Will's voice just as he's succeeding
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that creates dramatic irony - success turns to failure

jokes.text: I guess Will's coming with Boyz II Men. Do you think I should report my missing Santa suit to the police?
jokes.setup: Family assumes Will is arriving with the famous group
jokes.punchline: Phil worries about his stolen Santa suit, not realizing the connection
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony where audience knows connection but characters don't

jokes.text: I don't want my godson smelling that cheap perfume.
jokes.setup: Helen and Vy fighting over godmother position
jokes.punchline: Helen's petty complaint about Vy's perfume
jokes.explanation: Generic family argument escalation - functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Geoffrey! Geoffrey! Somebody spill something?
jokes.setup: The sisters dramatically call Geoffrey's name
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey assumes they need cleaning, not that he's being honored
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey character moment - his self-image as 'just the help' creates comedy

jokes.text: Oh, madam, I don't know what to say. This is the best Christmas gift I could ever get.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey is asked to be godfather
jokes.punchline: His overwhelming gratitude for being treated as family
jokes.explanation: Touching moment that resolves Geoffrey's Christmas struggles with genuine emotion

jokes.text: This is the best Christmas gift I could ever get. Thank you.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey is asked to be Nicky's godfather
jokes.punchline: His genuine emotional response to being treated as family
jokes.explanation: Heartwarming character moment that pays off Geoffrey's treatment subplot

jokes.text: Look, the truth is...
jokes.setup: Will addresses the church congregation about his promised gift
jokes.punchline: Begins to confess his lie but gets interrupted
jokes.explanation: Good tension moment building to climax resolution

jokes.text: Look, the truth is...
jokes.setup: Will about to confess his lie to the whole church
jokes.punchline: Cut off by Boyz II Men actually arriving
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing for the rescue - builds tension then releases it

jokes.text: Merry Christmas, Will.
jokes.setup: Will is about to confess his lie publicly
jokes.punchline: Boyz II Men arrive and save him from embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Perfect deus ex machina that subverts expected embarrassment with redemption

jokes.text: That's a good story, Daddy. Can I see my godfather now?
jokes.setup: Phil finishing the long elaborate story about the christening
jokes.punchline: Nicky politely dismissing it to see Geoffrey
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows what Nicky really cares about - not the story but Geoffrey

jokes.text: Now, get me some hot chocolate.
jokes.setup: Sweet moment between Nicky and Geoffrey as godfather/godson
jokes.punchline: Nicky immediately orders Geoffrey around like the rest of the family
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending callback that shows some things never change, even in sweet moments
